behavioralObligations.behavior: Every CE contest identifier is present in declaration order and selects the same contest family.
Every contest exposes CE-equivalent names, exchange types, defaults, and feature flags.
Every CE run mode is present and retains its observable selection semantics.
Certified parity always executes the pinned CE oracle and never silently substitutes a committed fixture.
The dedicated XPlat parity target executes every applicable acceptance case exactly once.
CI checks out the pinned clean CE revision and builds the hash-verified oracle before certification.
Every case retains complete replay inputs, red evidence, and a reachable first-green commit.
Manifest status, inputs, outputs, evidence classification, hashes, and Git provenance are internally consistent.
The inventory covers CE forms, projects, resources, generated files, tests, commands, data, and failure paths.
The CE oracle selects each ActiveContest before observing contest-dependent metadata or behavior.
UX, settings, data, adapters, and runtime features are certified by executed behavior rather than source tokens or filenames.
Local sidetone is mixed at the CE pipeline stage and level before filtering, modulation, and AGC.
Non-Farnsworth Morse symbols, cut numbers, unit timing, five millisecond ramps, and mark-space transitions match CE exactly.
Every CE moving-average receiver stage, reset, cascade order, and filter-swap numeric vector matches exactly.
Carrier frequency quantization, phase advance, quadrature modulation signs, and modulator numeric vectors match CE.
AGC attack, release, gain equations, state transitions, reset behavior, and output numeric vectors match CE.
Complex remote-station BFO mixing uses CE-equivalent signs, channel order, phase application, accumulation order, and output normalization.
The default CE compatibility output contract is 11025 Hz mono, 512 samples per block, and identical sample totals.
The physical audio queue preserves CE-equivalent default depth, FIFO block order, startup fill order, and exact sample continuity.
MT19937 output and every CE random distribution primitive produce deterministic numeric vectors for reviewed seeds.
QSK applies CE-equivalent receiver ducking and recovery around local transmissions.
RIT changes each station's audible offset using CE range, steps, and block-boundary timing.
Runtime bandwidth changes replace or update receiver filters at the CE-equivalent block boundary.
QRM uses CE-equivalent randomized interfering CW stations, messages, levels, pitches, speeds, retries, and lifetimes.
QRN produces CE sparse impulses and burst stations before receiver filtering and AGC.
Every remote station has an independent CE-distributed QSB process without fading the receiver noise floor.
Flutter is the CE probabilistic fast per-station QSB mode rather than a global multiplier.
Remote station amplitude and pitch use CE distributions and deterministic draw ordering.
Station BFO phase advances and resets at the same CE transmission boundaries.
The production keyer primitive used by SST supports independent character and spacing speeds with CE-equivalent marker encoding, sample counts, default ramps, and block padding.
Selecting SST carries the configured sending and character speeds through settings, session state, station message sequencing, and the production renderer so operator audio uses CE Farnsworth timing.
One seeded session random stream reproduces CE ownership and cross-feature draw order.
CE block sizes from 128 through 2048 are supported or proven import-equivalent with matching timing.
Startup warmup requests, block numbering, prefill, and alternating-filter swap timing match CE.
Base complex hiss and receiver noise floor match CE level, spectrum, random draws, and processing order.
WAV headers, PCM scaling, rounding, clipping, negative full scale, and sample bytes match CE vectors.
Recording failure and queue backpressure stop recording without faulting or allocating in the contest render path.
Raw capture, WAV, null, and physical playback receive identical ordered pre-conversion audio blocks.
Physical callbacks, underruns, drops, device loss, recovery, stale callbacks, shutdown, and disposal are bounded and proven natively.
All-effects rendering retains allocation, latency, queue-depth, underrun, drop, and GC evidence representative of sustained play.
Operator transmission completion and remote interpretation occur only after the rendered CW envelope completes.
Caller creation follows completed CQ, TU, and MyCall events with CE Poisson and no-stop rules.
Start is silent and an empty Enter initiates the appropriate CE contest CQ.
CQ, TU, operator call insertion, and periodic station-ID messages match each CE contest.
Remote exchanges retain and format every CE contest identity field, cut number, repeat, and correction variant.
CWT, SST, Field Day, NAQP, Sweepstakes, WPX, HST, and all other contests include every required CE exchange field.
ALLJA and ACAG station truth use the correct CE data columns without serial fallback.
Sweepstakes models serial, weighted precedence, own call, check, section, copied values, truth, and errors.

Help, About, readme, website, and community actions match CE availability, targets, errors, keyboard access, and focus return.
Score browsing, submission, disabled or unavailable service behavior, failures, focus, and history match CE.
Session lifecycle transitions reject invalid starts such as rmStop, define close-while-running behavior, and emit the same ordered terminal state through every client.
Every supported CE key is consumed with equivalent semantics or preserved losslessly across import, save, and restart.
CLI help, version, validation failures, runtime failures, and successful commands expose stable documented output and exit codes in packaged builds.
Legacy initialization and finalization hooks execute with equivalent order, registration, cleanup, state, and failures.

cases.behavior: For the fixed CE compatibility vector, narrowing receiver bandwidth from 500 Hz to 250 Hz between the first and second locally transmitted CQ blocks resets and reconfigures both receiver filters at the next block boundary without consuming random values or resetting modulator and AGC state.
preconditions: The pinned CE oracle and toolchain fingerprint are available.

Each target uses a fresh CQ WPX rmStop session with station call W7SST, seed 12345, 11025 Hz, 512 samples per block, 500 Hz initial bandwidth, 600 Hz pitch, 30 WPM, zero dB monitor level, no remote stations, and QRN, QRM, QSB, flutter, QSK, and LIDs disabled.

The CE target performs and verifies five one-Single startup requests, resets RandSeed to 12345 immediately before sending the real msgCQ text CQ W7SST TEST, and captures two normalized blocks through TContest.GetAudio.

The CE runtime calls TMainForm.SetBw with the reachable 250 Hz menu index after the first full request and before the second. Assigning TMovingAverage.Points resets both live filter histories, then SetBw applies the CE gain to both filters. Modulator and AGC state remain continuous.

Before implementation, the XPlat target starts two production manual-timing sessions, sends OperatorIntent.Cq, advances each session one block, and submits the production RadioControl.Bandwidth command only in the runtime-change path. The snapshot reaches 250 Hz while the active receiver filters remain configured at 500 Hz.
Both observations use the same authoritative terminal random value at ordinal 2048 because a bandwidth mutation consumes no random values.

The case pins one live 500 Hz to 250 Hz change and the immediately following local-transmission block. Wider transitions, repeated changes, filter-swap boundaries, remote receiver signals, client transport, and UX mutation remain pending, so the obligation remains partial.

assertions.firstDivergence: The zero-based first mismatch is ordinal 4, bandwidth-runtime-250-block[1]. CE calls TMainForm.SetBw before absolute block 7, resets and reconfigures both receiver filters, first diverges from fixed 500 Hz at sample 173, and emits hash 7c95262971abcf6acf2f0324fd5b7ffc33c46032ba66111709f445f6a9bd8275. XPlat accepts the bandwidth command and reports 250 Hz in the snapshot but leaves its receiver filters at 500 Hz, so it emits the fixed hash 98ed32d957fef5ee62e50a0a04cb063f4c027a9770d4a9e30b27c60dec52e234. Both terminal random checkpoints match.

cases.behavior: For the fixed seed 12345 vector, raw CE Random conversion to binary32, bounded integer Random including bound zero, and the CE RndUniform, RndUShaped, RndNormal, RndGaussLim, RndRayleigh, and RndPoisson routines produce the same first eight exact values, complete 4096-value distribution sequence hash, and post-group random-stream sentinel as the XPlat LegacyRandom and LegacyRandomEffects primitives.
preconditions: The pinned CE oracle and toolchain fingerprint are available.

The vector uses seed 12345, eight directly reported probes and 4096 total results per distribution group, integer bounds 0, 1, 2, 3, 10, 1000, 65536, and 2147483647, Gaussian mean 5.1 and limit 1.3, Rayleigh mean 2.7, and Poisson mean 3.3.
The CE adapter resets RandSeed to 12345 immediately before each raw or distribution group so every primitive starts from the same MT19937 state.
The raw group assigns each Random result directly to Single, and every floating-point result is compared by exact little-endian IEEE-754 binary32 bits.

The bounded-integer group executes the pinned FPC System.Random(LongInt) overload used by CE; this toolchain-owned overload has no RndFunc.pas inventory selector, while all CE distribution routines remain bound to their exact RndFunc selectors.
After each complete 4096-value group, the adapter records the next raw Random-to-Single value as a sentinel for the primitive's exact random draw consumption.
The XPlat adapter creates a fresh LegacyRandom for each group and invokes the production LegacyRandom and LegacyRandomEffects primitives without substituting recorded decisions.

assertions.firstDivergence: The zero-based first ordinal mismatch between fixture and observed string arrays, with null expected or actual only when one array ends first.

cases.behavior: For two fresh station-free CE receiver runs at the fixed compatibility vector with QSB disabled, enabling flutter changes neither of the first two normalized full audio blocks because CE uses flutter only while selecting a remote station's QSB bandwidth. The XPlat production engine must preserve the same exact receiver hiss and noise floor when flutter is enabled with no remote stations.
preconditions: The pinned CE oracle and toolchain fingerprint are available.

Each fresh CE runtime uses CQ WPX, rmStop, seed 12345, 11025 Hz, 512 samples per full block, 500 Hz bandwidth, 600 Hz pitch, no remote stations, no operator transmission, and QSB, QRM, QRN, and QSK disabled.
After contest construction, each CE run executes the real MainForm.SetBw path with handleless controls and resets RandSeed to 12345 immediately before its first GetAudio request.
Each CE run executes and verifies five real one-Single zero startup requests through TContest.GetAudio without resetting RandSeed before the two complete receiver blocks.

The two fresh CE runs differ only in Ini.Flutter. The station-free runtime constructs no TDxStation, and with Ini.Qsb false TContest.GetAudio reaches neither TDxStation.GetBlock nor TQsb.ApplyTo, so both normalized binary32 block sequences and their aggregate must remain bit-for-bit identical.

The XPlat adapter creates two fresh production engine sessions that differ only in SessionSettings.Flutter, keeps SessionSettings.Qsb false, starts and immediately aborts each session, verifies no audio before explicit advancement and zero active stations, then captures two complete blocks through IAudioSink.

This narrow case proves only that flutter does not multiply station-free receiver hiss. Positive per-station ownership, QSB gating, the 30 percent fast-mode selection, bandwidth distribution, draw order, independent envelopes, and runtime toggling remain uncovered, so audio.flutter-fast-per-station-qsb remains partial.

assertions.firstDivergence: The zero-based first mismatch is ordinal 3, flutter-block[0]. XPlat's session-global post-receiver flutter multiplier changes the station-free receiver block while CE leaves it identical to clean audio because CE flutter only selects a remote station's QSB bandwidth.

cases.behavior: At the fixed seed-24680 compatibility vector, an active K1ABC QRM station collides with ten scripted K1ABC caller candidates. CE constructs each complete TDxStation before collision testing, discards attempts 1 through 9, and accepts attempt 10 without a collision check. Every discarded candidate consumes its station, operator, speed, QSB, level, and pitch random sequence. The accepted caller retains the tenth catalog exchange and the shared random stream ends at ordinal 25373.
preconditions: The pinned CE v18 oracle and reviewed FPC 3.2.2 toolchain are available.
The fresh CE runtime uses CQ WPX, rmStop, seed 24680, 11025 Hz, 512 samples per block, station call W7SST, QRM enabled, and QRN, QSB, flutter, QSK, and LIDs disabled.
The scripted contest returns K1ABC for the active QRM station and for caller candidates 1 through 10. It assigns caller exchange rows EX01 through EX10 and records every completed GetExchange call.

TStations.AddCaller constructs a complete TDxStation before CallsignExists. Attempts 1 through 9 collide with the active QRM call and are freed. The retry counter reaches zero immediately after constructing attempt 10, so that final duplicate is accepted without calling CallsignExists.

Each TDxStation construction owns its R1, TDxOperator, receive speed, exchange, TQsb initialization and bandwidth refill, amplitude, and Gaussian pitch draws. Discarded candidates must therefore advance the authoritative shared stream exactly as live CE candidates do.

The internal XPlat probe requires manual timing, exact session revision and block, QRM enabled, and a fresh one-shot session. It runs on the session worker, creates one production QrmStation, invokes production AddCaller with a scripted catalog selector, and is absent from IMorseRunnerClient, snapshots, gRPC, and UX contracts.

This case certifies active-QRM collision visibility, complete-candidate retry construction, nine discards, unconditional tenth-attempt acceptance, exact retained exchange/operator fields, and the terminal random checkpoint. It does not certify general collision rates, non-QRM caller collisions, other contests, runtime audio mixing, or caller lifecycle after acceptance.

assertions.firstDivergence: The zero-based first mismatch is ordinal 2, catalog. The pre-implementation XPlat engine checks collisions only against normal callers, accepts the first K1ABC identity beside the active K1ABC QRM source, and constructs only that accepted caller after selection. CE selects eleven catalog rows total, fully constructs ten caller candidates, discards nine, and unconditionally accepts the tenth.

cases.behavior: At the fixed seed-1843 compatibility vector, the first live CE receiver block satisfies the QRM trigger and eagerly creates exactly one internal TQrmStation. The constructor consumes the shared random stream through message selection, selects LU5MT from the content-bound MASTER.DTA catalog, sends QRL?   QRL? at 31 WPM with the pinned amplitude and pitch, contributes the first 512 samples of its 53248-sample envelope to the same receiver block, and remains stSending. The XPlat production engine must reproduce the source-order random ownership, internal station metadata, message, same-block receiver output, and terminal checkpoint without exposing QRM as a public caller.
preconditions: The pinned CE oracle, toolchain fingerprint, and content-bound MASTER.DTA catalog are available.

Each fresh CE runtime uses CQ WPX, rmStop, seed 1843, 11025 Hz, 512 samples per complete block, 500 Hz bandwidth, 600 Hz pitch, station call W7SST, no normal DX stations, no operator transmission, and QRN, QSB, flutter, QSK, and LIDs disabled.

After contest construction, each CE run executes the real MainForm.SetBw path with handleless controls, resets RandSeed to 1843 immediately before audio framing, verifies five real one-Single zero startup requests, and captures exactly one complete receiver block.

The fresh CE runs differ only in Ini.Qrm. The complete block consumes 1024 complex-hiss draws before the QRM trigger at ordinal 1024. Seed 1843 yields binary32 bits 38e1bf40, which satisfies the 0.0002 creation threshold.

The live constructor consumes R1 ordinal 1025, patience ordinal 1026, catalog ordinal 1027, amplitude ordinal 1028, Gaussian pitch ordinals 1029 and 1030, WPM ordinal 1031, and message ordinal 1032. The next shared-stream checkpoint is ordinal 1033.
The content-bound MASTER.DTA is 1239476 bytes with SHA-256 acf37090e7c9c0f2146a2b08608295cb243c8bfe649a421d1c528a59656097aa and 46039 calls. Catalog index 23903 selects LU5MT.

The live TQrmStation has patience 2, R1 bits 3f03301e, amplitude 19583.306640625 with bits 4698fe9d, pitch offset -124 Hz, WpmS and WpmC 31, and message choice 2 selecting [msgQrl2] with text QRL?   QRL?.

The message encoder eagerly creates a 53248-sample, 104-block envelope. Station.GetBlock contributes its first 512 samples in the trigger block, advances SendPos to 512, leaves 103 complete blocks, and retains the station in stSending.

The clean normalized block hash is 3ba44162f2959aeeaa6599059e97033d942258e3a2e0dc33cbb07defbb12a4c5. The QRM block hash is 72f7618e7e055db7fefd472c47f0488046087905d5810b1e1aa97b88187f643d, with first final-output divergence at sample 310.

The XPlat adapter creates independent clean and QRM-enabled production MorseRunnerEngine sessions with automatic timing disabled, starts and immediately aborts each rmStop session, advances exactly one block, captures actual IAudioSink output, validates public caller snapshots and events remain empty, obtains one guarded internal QRM observation, and then reads the authoritative terminal random checkpoint.

The QRM observation seam is internal and parity-only. It requires manual timing plus exact session revision and simulation block, runs on the session worker, consumes no random value, advances no simulation state, and is absent from IMorseRunnerClient, public snapshots, gRPC, and UX surfaces. Before QRM production exists it returns an explicit empty observation.

This case certifies one first-block successful trigger, eager constructor draw order, one catalog selection, one level, pitch, speed, message, envelope length, same-block audibility, and terminal checkpoint. It does not certify trigger-rate statistics, other message branches or distributions, retries, timeout Gaussian behavior, complete lifetime, overlaps, normal-station interaction, RIT, or runtime toggling, so audio.qrm-interfering-cw-stations remains partial.

assertions.firstDivergence: The zero-based first mismatch is ordinal 3, station. XPlat replays the content-bound CE constructor decisions and captures the exact clean receiver path, but production does not yet create an internal QRM station when ordinal 1024 satisfies the trigger, so the guarded parity observation reports zero instead of one TQrmStation. The QRM block consequently remains equal to clean rather than first diverging at final-output sample 310, and the shared stream does not advance through constructor ordinal 1032.

cases.behavior: For two fresh station-free CE receiver runs at the fixed compatibility vector with QRN disabled, seed 12345 does not satisfy the first complete block's QRM construction trigger, so enabling QRM creates no TQrmStation and changes neither the normalized full audio block nor its raw-Single hash. The XPlat production engine must preserve the same exact receiver output when QRM is enabled but no interfering station is triggered.
preconditions: The pinned CE oracle and toolchain fingerprint are available.

Each fresh CE runtime uses CQ WPX, rmStop, seed 12345, 11025 Hz, 512 samples per full block, 500 Hz bandwidth, 600 Hz pitch, no normal DX stations, no operator transmission, and QSB, flutter, QRN, QSK, and LIDs disabled.
After contest construction, each CE run executes the real MainForm.SetBw path with handleless controls and resets RandSeed to 12345 immediately before its first GetAudio request.
Each CE run executes and verifies five real one-Single zero startup requests through TContest.GetAudio without resetting RandSeed before exactly one complete receiver block.

The two fresh CE runs differ only in Ini.Qrm. With Ini.Qrn false, the QRM construction trigger consumes shared random ordinal 1024 after the block's 1024 hiss draws. Seed 12345 yields approximately 0.17384081427007914, so the real production station collection must remain empty in both runs and the two normalized binary32 blocks must be bit-for-bit identical.
No second block is compared because the QRM-enabled run consumed the otherwise absent trigger draw and therefore begins its next block at a different shared-stream position.

The XPlat adapter creates two fresh production MorseRunnerEngine and EngineSession sessions that differ only in SessionSettings.Qrm, starts and immediately aborts each session, verifies no audio before one explicit simulation-block advance and zero active stations, then captures the complete block through the actual IAudioSink.

This narrow case proves only no-trigger output invariance. Positive QRM construction, construction probability and draw ownership, a shared-random sentinel, messages, levels, pitch, WPM, same-block audibility, retries, and lifetime remain uncovered, so audio.qrm-interfering-cw-stations remains partial.

assertions.firstDivergence: The zero-based first mismatch is ordinal 2, qrm-block[0]. XPlat's unconditional post-receiver QRM sine changes the station-free receiver block while CE adds no TQrmStation and leaves the first complete block identical to clean audio because seed 12345 does not satisfy the QRM trigger.

cases.behavior: For two fresh station-free CE receiver runs at the fixed compatibility vector, enabling QRN consumes one trigger draw per raw complex-noise sample, replaces the real component at the six source-order replay-derived indexes 92, 248, 323, 482, 488, and 507, consumes one value draw for each replacement, and consumes a final burst trigger that creates no TQrnStation. The live QRN receiver output differs from clean output first at normalized sample 310 after CE receiver filtering, modulation, and AGC. The XPlat production engine must reproduce the exact clean and QRN blocks, station counts, and terminal shared-stream sentinels.
preconditions: The pinned CE oracle and toolchain fingerprint are available.
